subject: Mozi Minute: SPCL Influence
body:
Words I like: The whole point of marketing is to become trusted. Once you're trusted, you don't need anything else.

Mozi Minute: SPCL Influence

Many business owners make random content hoping to gain influence. But they don't actually know what they're doing. And that's fine.

But, if you'd like to get more out for what you put in (leverage), here's how I think about it.

Influence = the likelihood someone follows your directions

The elements that increase influence = SPCL

Status = you control things the prospect finds valuable. Ex = you have money and you can demonstrate it.

Power = they followed your directions in the past and had a good thing happen. Ex = you say to add a 2-step optin to increase optin rates. They do it. Then it does. So the next time you give a directive they are more likely to follow it.

Credibility = you have proof. Ex = you've won awards, or have visual proof, around whatever thing you're telling them to do.

Likeness = do you look and act like them. This doesn't have to be physical, but physically looking similar will increase influence. This is why advertising campaigns benefit from multiple different avatars.

Note: some of these can be accomplished at the same time.

Ex: me selling a company provides both status and credibility. I sold the company which gives me credibility. I got lots of money for it which gives status.

Alternatively, I could just have money which would not give me credibility for selling a company (ex = trust fund kid inherits money).

Or, I could have sold a company but got paid almost nothing because I had diluted my shares which would not give me status (ex = founder who takes on tons of debt and leaves empty handed but knows how to scale).

So next time you make content...

Try to show you have what they want, give them directions on how to get it, show proof of 3rd party or observable things you could only achieve with following the advice you're giving, be yourself (and you'll attract people like you).

As promised, value in under a minute.

Build a reputation then protect it with your life,

Alex
